description:
You are the release manager preparing artifacts for the next deployment.  
    A small “data-pipeline” style routine must be performed that (1) reads a manifest,  
    (2) copies every package listed in the manifest from the *source* directory to the *staging* directory,  
    (3) automatically skips any package that does **not** exist in the source directory, and  
    (4) writes a concise log summarising what happened.

    The working directories and files already present on the system are:

    1. /home/user/release/manifest.txt  
       • Plain-text file that contains one filename per line (see exact contents in the truth section).  
    2. /home/user/packages/               ── source directory  
       • Contains a couple of “.tar.gz” package archives.  
    3. /home/user/staging/                ── destination directory  
       • Initially empty.

    Your goals:

    A. Copy every **existing** package named in the manifest from /home/user/packages/ into /home/user/staging/.  
       • Do **not** halt if one of the packages mentioned in the manifest is missing; simply skip it.  

    B. Produce a human-readable single log file at  
       /home/user/release/deployment_report_2024-01-01.txt  
       that records which packages were successfully copied and which were missing.

       Log file format (the automated tests are case- and whitespace-sensitive):  

       Line 1:   Deployment Report  
       Line 2:   Date: 2024-01-01  

       Line 3:   (blank)  
       Line 4:   SUCCESS:  
       Line 5+:  one filename per line for every package that was copied, in the same order they appear in the manifest.  

       After the last successful filename there is a single blank line, then the literal header “ERRORS:” followed by one filename per line for every package listed in the manifest that was **not** found in /home/user/packages/.  
       There is a final trailing newline at the end of the file.

       Concrete example of the required shape (filenames will differ):  
       ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  
       Deployment Report  
       Date: 2024-01-01  

       SUCCESS:  
       foo-1.0.0.tar.gz  
       bar-2.0.0.tar.gz  

       ERRORS:  
       baz-3.0.0.tar.gz  
       ─────────────────────────────────────────────────  

    C. After the pipeline is run, /home/user/staging/ must contain only the packages that existed in /home/user/packages/ and were listed in the manifest.  

    Practical constraints:

    • Accomplish everything with at most four separate shell commands.  
    • You do **not** have root access. Do not use sudo.  
    • Do not modify the manifest itself.  
    • The log file must be created exactly once; overwriting is fine, appending is not.  

    Once finished, the automated grader will check:  
    1. That /home/user/staging/ has the correct files.  
    2. That /home/user/release/deployment_report_2024-01-01.txt exists and its content matches the strict format above.

    Hints you may find useful (but totally optional):  
    • `grep`, `while read`, `printf`, `tee`, `cp --preserve=mode` are all available.  
    • Pay attention to blank lines: they matter in the diff.  
    • Absolute paths keep things unambiguous.

    Good luck, Release Manager!
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent begins:

    Directories:
        /home/user/release/
        /home/user/packages/
        /home/user/staging/

    Files already present:

    1. /home/user/release/manifest.txt  
       Exact contents (4 lines, each terminated by a single `\n`):  
       app1-1.0.0.tar.gz  
       app2-2.1.0.tar.gz  
       app3-3.0.0.tar.gz  
       readme.txt  

    2. /home/user/packages/app1-1.0.0.tar.gz         (non-empty dummy file)  
    3. /home/user/packages/app2-2.1.0.tar.gz         (non-empty dummy file)  
       (app3-3.0.0.tar.gz and readme.txt do **not** exist in /home/user/packages/.)

    4. /home/user/staging/                           (empty)

    Expected final state after successful task execution:

    A. /home/user/staging/ contains **only**  
       • app1-1.0.0.tar.gz  
       • app2-2.1.0.tar.gz  

    B. /home/user/release/deployment_report_2024-01-01.txt exists with the following exact bytes (each line ends with `\n`):

       Deployment Report\n
       Date: 2024-01-01\n
       \n
       SUCCESS:\n
       app1-1.0.0.tar.gz\n
       app2-2.1.0.tar.gz\n
       \n
       ERRORS:\n
       app3-3.0.0.tar.gz\n
       readme.txt\n

    Permissions: all files are writable by user “user” (default umask 022 is acceptable).
